title: DANTE'S OFFICE
subtitle: Static Bloom · Vol 5 · Ch IV · The Emperor
notes: Dante's office on the bank, three doors down from where Nicola's boat is moored. A small, formal room with a heavy desk, two chairs facing it, a filing cabinet that goes from floor to ceiling, and a window onto the river. People come here when they need something put in writing. Nine spaces; tight, deliberate, every room has a purpose.
ambient_pool: the brass desk clock ticks. it has been ticking since dante's father had this office.
the radiator is on too high. dante hasn't touched the dial all winter.
the river is visible through the office window. nicola's boat is barely in the frame.
the filing cabinet smells faintly of carbon paper and dust.
outside, somebody on the bank is talking to themselves.
the stamp on the desk leaves a perfect circle, every time, for forty years.

spaces.label: FRONT DOOR
spaces.flavor: the office's street-facing door — clients walk through here.
arrival_lines: the bell above the door hasn't been replaced. it still rings the way it did in 1962.
the doorway is taller than it needs to be. it's been that tall on purpose.

spaces.label: ALLEY DOOR
spaces.flavor: the back door, onto the alley behind the bank-side buildings.
arrival_lines: the alley smells like rain even when there's no rain.
the door's hinges complain. they have complained for as long as the bell has rung.

spaces.label: WINDOW LEDGE
spaces.flavor: the office window — the third leap path, onto the lower roof of the building next door.
arrival_lines: the ledge is wide enough for a person who decides quickly.
the roof next door is two feet down. you could be on the bank in twenty steps.

spaces.label: DESK
spaces.flavor: the desk · dante's chair · stamp, blotter, the working ledger.
arrival_lines: the desk is heavier than it needs to be. it has been moved exactly once, in 1979.
the stamp is to your right. the ledger is open.

spaces.label: CLIENT CHAIRS
spaces.flavor: the two chairs that face the desk · where the petitioners sit.
arrival_lines: two chairs. they have been the same two chairs for the entire office's existence.
the seat has the shape of every person who has sat in it.

spaces.label: FILING CABINET
spaces.flavor: wall-to-ceiling files · dante's father's hand, dante's hand, alphabetised.
arrival_lines: the cabinet smells like carbon paper. the smell is on you now too.
you find a folder you don't remember filing. the date is yesterday.

spaces.label: LAW SHELF
spaces.flavor: the bookcase · statute, precedent, the small private collection.
arrival_lines: the law books are not all law books. one of them is a poetry collection his mother loved.
the third shelf has volumes 7, 8, and 11 of the river-code · 9 and 10 are missing.

spaces.label: ANTEROOM
spaces.flavor: the small room where clients wait when dante is with somebody else.
arrival_lines: the anteroom has two chairs and a small table with a yesterday's newspaper.
the wall here is thin. you can hear a low voice in the next room.

spaces.label: STAMP TABLE
spaces.flavor: a smaller workbench by the window · where the heavy stamps live.
arrival_lines: the heavy stamps are bronze. they outweigh their cases.
each stamp has a date-wheel. one of them is set to a date that hasn't happened yet.

hand_overrides._notes: Guest-lens overrides. The board geometry is Dante's office, but when john_frank is the hand, every space reads as the back office of D'Ambrosio's — same rooms, different vocabulary. This is the proxy-lens mechanic in JSON form: same map, character-specific labels and flavor.

john_frank._lens_notes: John has been called into the back office during lunch-rush prep. Sal (the owner) is at the desk. The schedule book is on the wall. The line cooks have the door propped open with a milk crate so they can hear him through it. The brass desk clock is the same clock Dante's office has because the building's the same building — D'Ambrosio's owns the bank-side storefront and Sal inherited the clock with the lease in '88.

front_door.label: FRONT DOOR
front_door.flavor: the door to the diner floor — coworkers come and go through here, with the smell of the grill in their hair.
arrival_lines: the bell above the door is the bell above d'ambrosio's front door, two rooms away. you hear it when somebody walks in for a counter seat.
the door is propped open with a milk crate. the line cooks did that. you suspect frank.

alley_door.label: BACK ALLEY
alley_door.flavor: the smoke-break exit — the dish station is on the other side of it.
arrival_lines: the alley smells like the grease trap. it always has. you grew up on this smell.
the door's hinges complain. they have complained since you started here in '02.

window_ledge.label: WINDOW LEDGE
window_ledge.flavor: the back-office window — onto the alley and the lower roof of the music store. for the day you actually walk out.
arrival_lines: the ledge is wide enough for somebody who decides quickly. you have not decided.
the music store's lower roof is two feet down. you could be off the block in twenty steps.

desk.label: SAL'S DESK
desk.flavor: the owner's desk · sal's chair · the schedule book, the stamp, the ledger that's actually a payroll ledger.
arrival_lines: sal's desk. the chair is empty for now. the schedule book is open to next week.
the stamp is to the right of the blotter. john has never seen sal use it.

client_chairs.label: COWORKER CHAIRS
client_chairs.flavor: the two chairs facing the desk · where the line cooks sit when they come in to complain.
arrival_lines: two chairs. frank has worn the cushion of the left one flat.
the right chair is the one nobody takes because sal looks at you harder from this angle.

filing_cabinet.label: PERSONNEL FILES
filing_cabinet.flavor: the cabinet of personnel folders + the time-clock book · sal's hand, sal's father's hand · alphabetised.
arrival_lines: your folder is in there. you have never opened it. you can guess what it says.
the time-clock book is open to this month. your hours are clean except the wednesday you swapped with elena.

law_shelf.label: EMPLOYEE HANDBOOK
law_shelf.flavor: the shelf of three-ring binders · corporate handbook, last updated '87, nobody reads it.
arrival_lines: the handbook smells like the eighties. the pages are still crisp because nobody ever opened them.
section 7 covers schedule conflicts. you have read section 7 four times in the last month.

anteroom.label: BREAK ROOM
anteroom.flavor: the small room where the staff waits when sal is with somebody else · couch, fridge, the coffeemaker that's been on since 1991.
arrival_lines: the break room has the magazine that has been on the table since march.
the wall is thin. you can hear sal through the door when he's on the phone.

stamp_table.label: PUNCH CLOCK
stamp_table.flavor: the punch clock + the wall schedule with magnets · where shifts get decided.
arrival_lines: the magnets on the schedule board are color-coded by station. john's magnet is dish-blue this week.
the punch clock has a date wheel set to today. it has been a quarter-hour fast since '04.

ledger_track.label: LEDGER
ledger_track.notes: Dante's positive doom-clock. Each WITNESS / STAMP action enters a ruling into the working ledger and ticks it. At threshold, the day's docket is complete. Some scenarios use the ledger as a soft win counter, some as a hard limit (the ledger filling means the office closes for the day).

ladder.label: the ledger is open to today's date.
two rulings. the day is starting.
halfway · the heavy stamps come out.
the docket is complete. the office closes.
